metadata.title: Well Da Factory Limited
metadata.description: Well Da Factory Limited — precision metal manufacturing, CNC machining, and custom fabrication in Hong Kong.

search.noQuery: Enter a search term to find products, capabilities, FAQ answers, and more.

sections.title: Custom Metal Enclosures
sections.description: Custom-fabricated metal housings for electronic equipment, control systems, industrial devices and specialised applications.

sections.title: Electrical and Control Boxes
sections.description: Sheet metal electrical boxes and control enclosures manufactured according to customer drawings, installation requirements and operating environments.

sections.title: Cabinets and Lockers
sections.description: Custom metal cabinets, equipment cabinets and storage lockers designed for industrial, commercial and security applications.

sections.title: Communication Equipment Housings
sections.description: Metal cabinets and enclosures for communication equipment, network hardware and related infrastructure.

sections.title: Security System Hardware
sections.description: Custom metal housings, brackets and supporting components for security, access-control and anti-theft systems.

sections.title: Non-Standard Metal Products
sections.description: Made-to-order sheet metal products for projects that cannot be fulfilled with standard off-the-shelf components.

manufacturing.intro: Our manufacturing capabilities cover the key stages required to produce custom metal products, from material preparation and forming to finishing and final assembly.

manufacturing.materials: We work with steel, stainless steel, electrolytic steel sheet and aluminium according to project requirements.

quality.sectionTitle: Controlled Quality from Drawing Review to Final Inspection
paragraphs: Every project begins with a review of the drawing, material, dimensions, surface finish and application requirements.
For suitable sheet metal components, manufacturing precision of up to **±0.2 mm** may be achieved, depending on product design, material, thickness and production process.
Inspection requirements can include dimensional checks, welding quality, surface finishing, assembly and final appearance.

sections.title: From Flat Sheet to Finished Electrical Enclosure
sections.description:
For this custom electrical box project, the production process included sheet cutting, punching or laser opening, bending, welding, grinding, powder coating, silk-screen printing and final assembly.

By coordinating each manufacturing stage around the confirmed drawing, the enclosure was produced according to the required dimensions, appearance and functional requirements.

images.alt: Custom electrical enclosure manufactured from flat sheet metal, showing the final assembled product
Finished electrical enclosure with powder-coated finish and silk-screen printing

sections.title: Ordering Kiosk Enclosure
sections.description: A rugged, purpose-built enclosure designed for self-service ordering kiosks. The cabinet features integrated cable management routing and efficient ventilation to support internal hardware components. Manufactured to the client's exact 42U rack-mount specifications, all panels are produced through precision laser cutting, CNC bending, structural welding, and a durable powder-coated finish—ensuring long-lasting durability and a clean, professional appearance suitable for public-facing environments.

images.alt: Custom ordering kiosk enclosure with integrated cable management routing and ventilation
Ordering kiosk enclosure showing rack-mount design and powder-coated finish

quotation.heading: Have a Drawing or Custom Metal Project?
quotation.intro: Send us your drawing, CAD, DXF or STEP file together with the required material, thickness, quantity, surface finish and delivery schedule. Our team will review the manufacturing requirements and prepare a quotation based on your project specifications.
quotation.contactIntro: Contact us to get started — choose the method that works best for you:

about.profileDesc1: Custom metal enclosures and sheet metal products made with specified materials, proven workmanship and careful quality control.
about.profileDesc2: From electrical boxes and equipment cabinets to non-standard metal components, we manufacture according to your confirmed drawings and project requirements.
about.sendDrawingDesc: Share your CAD files, blueprints, or sketches — our engineering team will review your specifications and provide feedback.
about.coreTitle: Flexible Manufacturing for Custom and Non-Standard Projects
about.coreDesc1: We support prototype, low-volume and production orders with flexible manufacturing, practical engineering communication and a complete range of sheet metal processes.
about.coreDesc2: Whether you have a finished drawing, a sample or an initial product concept, our team can help evaluate the manufacturing requirements and recommend a suitable production approach.
about.trustDesc: Our credentials and capabilities reflect decades of dedication to quality and precision in metal manufacturing.

questions.question: What types of products do you manufacture?
answer: We manufacture custom sheet metal parts, enclosures, housings, cabinets, panels and non-standard metal products based on customer drawings or design requirements.
Typical applications include intercom systems, ordering kiosks, electrical equipment, communication systems, security equipment, lockers and precision equipment housings.

questions.question: Do you have a minimum order quantity?
answer: There is no fixed minimum order quantity.
We can support single-piece prototypes, small-batch orders and production quantities. Pricing will depend on the material, manufacturing process, setup requirements and order quantity.

questions.question: What drawing formats do you accept?
answer: We can review common engineering drawing and 3D file formats, including:
Please include dimensions, material, thickness, tolerance, surface finish and quantity whenever possible.

questions.question: Can you manufacture from a sample?
answer: Sample-based evaluation may be possible, depending on the product.
However, drawings or dimensional information are strongly recommended to improve quotation accuracy and reduce the risk of misunderstanding.

questions.question: What materials can you work with?
answer: Material grade and thickness should be specified in the drawing or enquiry. Alternative materials may be reviewed case by case.

questions.question: What manufacturing processes are available?
answer: The final manufacturing route will be selected according to the product design, material, tolerance and order quantity.

questions.question: What tolerance can you achieve?
answer: Depending on the material, part geometry and manufacturing process, dimensional tolerance may reach approximately **±0.2 mm**.
Not every dimension can be controlled to the same tolerance. Critical dimensions and inspection requirements should be clearly identified on the drawing for engineering review.

questions.question: Can you produce prototypes?
answer: Yes. We can support prototypes and trial production before mass production.
Prototype lead time is commonly around one week, depending on material availability, product complexity, finishing requirements and engineering confirmation.

questions.question: What is the typical production lead time?
answer: Lead time depends on product complexity, quantity, material availability and surface treatment.
Simple orders may be completed in approximately 10 working days, while larger production orders commonly require around one month, including sample approval where applicable.
The confirmed lead time will be stated in the quotation.

questions.question: What surface finishes are available?
answer: Colour references, surface standards and appearance requirements should be provided before production.

questions.question: Can you manufacture outdoor or weather-resistant products?
answer: We can manufacture products with outdoor anti-rust and water-resistance requirements through material selection, structural design, welding, sealing and surface treatment.
However, an IP rating or other certified protection level should only be claimed after appropriate testing.
Third-party testing may be arranged on a project basis, subject to the customer's requirements, budget and testing standard.

questions.question: Are you ISO certified?
answer: Our manufacturing operation follows an ISO 9001 quality management system.
Relevant certification information may be provided during supplier qualification or project discussion.

questions.question: How do you control product quality?
answer: Quality control may include incoming material checking, dimensional inspection, in-process inspection, appearance checking and final inspection before shipment.
Inspection requirements, critical dimensions and acceptance criteria should be confirmed before production.

questions.question: Can you help improve my design for manufacturing?
answer: Yes. Our engineering team can review drawings for manufacturability and may suggest adjustments relating to bending, hole positions, welding access, material use, tolerances, assembly and production efficiency.
Any design changes will be discussed with the customer before production.

questions.question: Can you arrange international shipping?
answer: Yes. Depending on the shipment size and destination, delivery may be arranged by courier, air freight or sea freight.
Customers may also nominate their own freight forwarder or arrange factory collection.
Freight, customs duties, taxes and import clearance responsibilities will be confirmed separately.

questions.question: What information is required for a quotation?
answer: For a more accurate quotation, please provide:
Incomplete information may result in a preliminary quotation that is subject to engineering confirmation.

questions.question: How quickly can I receive a quotation?
answer: Quotation time depends on the complexity and completeness of the enquiry.
Straightforward parts may be reviewed quickly, while assemblies, tight-tolerance products or projects requiring material and process evaluation may require additional engineering review.
Providing complete drawings and requirements will help shorten the quotation process.
